task_completion.description: The QR code image is displayed on screen to be captured by the virtual camera.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OBS Studio (or an image viewer/other software) is open and displaying the totp_setup.png QR code.

task_completion.description: The bcWebCam application is launched and visible on the screen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bcWebCam application window is open on the desktop.

task_completion.description: bcWebCam is actively receiving the virtual camera feed containing the QR code.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The QR code is visible inside the live camera feed area of the bcWebCam application interface.

task_completion.description: bcWebCam successfully scans the QR code and outputs the URI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'otpauth://' URI is visible in the scanned text output box within the bcWebCam interface.

task_completion.description: The authenticator script is run with the extracted secret.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Command Prompt or PowerShell window shows the command 'python C:\Users\Docker\Documents\authenticator.py add CorpVPN <secret>' where <secret> is a Base32 string.

integrity.description: The agent used bcWebCam to decode the QR code as requested, rather than a CLI tool or script.
integrity.visual_evidence: The decoded 'otpauth://' URI appears in the bcWebCam GUI, not just as output from a Python/ZBar script in the terminal.

integrity.description: The agent extracted ONLY the Base32 secret from the URI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal command shows only the Base32 string (e.g., ABCDEF...) being passed to the script, without 'otpauth://', '?secret=', or other URI parameters.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely interacted with the required GUI applications.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual windows for OBS Studio (or similar) and bcWebCam open and configured, rather than just terminal windows.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected account name to be added via the script is 'CorpVPN'.
